title: Chromolaena cylindrocephala
intro: Chromolaena cylindrocephala, also known as the cylindrical flower bush, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena cylindrocephala include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies, including teas, capsules, and topical creams.
taxonomy: Chromolaena cylindrocephala is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America, and its classification places it within the genus Chromolaena. Chromolaena cylindrocephala is closely related to other members of the Chromolaena genus, which is often referred to as a group of "tea shrubs." The plant is considered an invasive species in some regions. Chromolaena cylindrocephala is also known by its synonyms, Chromolaena scorzonerifolia and Eupatorium scorzonerifolium.
